q)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>definition</b></font><font where</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> &quot;</font><font New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> pop :: &quot;(&prime;a, &prime;b::linorder) pri_queue ⇒ (&prime;a × (&prime;a, &prime;b) pri_queue) option&quot;</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> where</b></font><font Roman;color:#0000ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">pop </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">q = (if is_empty q <br/> then None else Some (min q, remove_min q))&quot;<br/><br/>## Theorem statement <br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>theorem</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> pop_remove_elememt_notin_queue: assumes a1:&quot;pop q = Some (v, q1)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>shows </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;v \&lt;notin&gt; set (values q1)&quot;<br/>```<br/>## Informal proof<br/>Theorem: Given pop q = Some (v, q1), we want to show that v \&lt;notin&gt; set (values q1).<br/>Proof:<br/>Step1: Case Analysis on is_empty q:<br/>Step2: Case 1: Assume is_empty q = True.<br/> In this case, by the definition of pop, pop q should be None. This contradicts our assumption that pop q = Some (v, q1). ...<br/>Step3: Case 2: Assume is_empty q = False.<br/> Here, by the definition of pop, we have pop q = Some (min q, remove_min q). Therefore, from our assumption, it follows that q1 = remove_min q...<br/>Step4: Unfold the values Function for q1: Using the definition of values, we have: values (q1) = values (remove_min q) = map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq...<br/>Step5: By the properties of the Abs_pq constructor and the alist_of function, particularly the inverse relationship between Abs_pq and alist_of, it <br/> follows that: map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq (tl (alist_of q)))) = map fst (tl (alist_of q)) Hence, values(q1) = map fst (tl (alist_of q)).<br/>Step7: Distinctness and the Minimum Element:<br/> Since the alist_of q list is distinct and sorted, the first element hd (alist_of q) is not in the tail of the list tl (alist_of q).Therefore, the first component ..<br/>Step8: Conclusion: Combining the above results, we conclude that v \&lt;notin&gt; set (values q1), which completes the proof.<br/><br/>[2 more examples...]<br/><br/>## Theorem statement <br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>theorem</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> min_priority_leq_all: <br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>assumes </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">a1:&quot;¬ is_empty q&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>and </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">a2:&quot;v ∈ set |q|&quot;<br/> shows &quot;the (priority q (min q)) ≤ the (priority q v)&quot;<br/><br/>Output:<br/>## Informal proof<br/>Proof Step:<br/>To prove this theorem, we proceed step by step:<br/>Step 1: Definitions Recap:<br/> Let&prime;s recap the definitions of the relevant functions and properties:<br/> - min Function: The min function returns the minimum value in the priority queue q. It is defined as `min q = fst (hd (alist_of q))`. Here, `hd` ...<br/>Step 2: Proof Breakdown:<br/> To prove the theorem, we need to show that the priority of the minimum value in q is less than or equal to the priority of v.<br/>Step 3: Case Analysis:<br/> We will perform a case analysis based on the assumption a1: `\&lt;not&gt; is_empty q`. Since q is not empty, there are two possible cases:<br/> Case 1: q is empty: If q is empty according to the assumption, then it contradicts the premise that v is in the set of values of q (a2: `v \&lt;in&gt; set |q|`). ...<br/> Case 2: q is not empty:If q is not empty, then the minimum value in q can be obtained using the min function: `min q`. Let&prime;s denote this minimum ....<br/>Step 4: Priority of m and v:<br/> We want to show that the priority of m is less than or equal to the priority of v. We can express this as: `the (priority q m) \&lt;le&gt; the (priority q v)`.<br/>Step 5: Unfolding priority:<br/> Using the definition of the priority function, we can rewrite the inequality as: `the (map_of (alist_of q) m) \&lt;le&gt; the (map_of (alist_of q) v)`.<br/>Step 6: Set Membership:<br/> Since v is in the set of values of q according to the assumption a2, it implies that v is present in the list representation of q, which is given by ...<br/>Step 7: Map_of Behavior:The map_of function returns None if the key v is not<br/></font></p></div>" tags="Background" id="ThWJSYYnvA3JDk2Efs-2-2"> <mxCell parent="1" style="verticalAlign=middle;align=left;overflow=width;vsdxID=2;fillColor=none;gradientColor=none;shape=stencil(nZBRDoQgDERP03+WHsHFe5iISyOCQdT19kIaIxq//Jtp3zTpAFaTaUYNUkwx+F6v1EYD+AUpyRkdKCYFqACrzgf9C352LfuxyWRWg1/yhT/nRE5IsZWuZtCSK8CPeI0e/hF+jV7AJM4PO7KWCyr390bSiNtEtQM=);strokeColor=none;spacingTop=-3;spacingBottom=-3;spacingLeft=-3;spacingRight=-3;points=[];labelBackgroundColor=none;rounded=0;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="922" width="730" x="794" y="283" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </UserObject> <UserObject label="" tags="Background" id="ThWJSYYnvA3JDk2Efs-2-3"> <mxCell edge="1" parent="1" style="vsdxID=3;edgeStyle=none;dashed=1;dashPattern=4.00 2.00;startArrow=none;endArrow=none;startSize=5;endSize=5;strokeWidth=2;strokeColor=#000000;spacingTop=0;spacingBottom=0;spacingLeft=0;spacingRight=0;verticalAlign=middle;html=1;labelBackgroundColor=#ffffff;rounded=0;"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="-359" y="102" as="offset" /> <Array as="points" /> <mxPoint x="796" y="933" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="1513" y="933" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> </UserObject> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

p.font:
Input:
You are an expert in theorem proving.your task is to analyze the given theorem (including the problem’s function definitions, and formal statement).
Provide a better structured step-by-step proof .
## Function definitions

p.font:
k p q ≡ Abs_pq (if k ∉ set (values q)
then insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q)else alist_of q)"

p.font:
q ≡ (if is_empty q
then empty else Abs_pq (tl (alist_of q)))"

p.font:
q = (if is_empty q
then None else Some (min q, remove_min q))"
## Theorem statement
```isabelle

p.font: pop_remove_elememt_notin_queue: assumes a1:"pop q = Some (v, q1)"

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 153, 102), rgb(37, 168, 125)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
"v \<notin> set (values q1)"
```
## Informal proof
Theorem: Given pop q = Some (v, q1), we want to show that v \<notin> set (values q1).
Proof:
Step1: Case Analysis on is_empty q:
Step2: Case 1: Assume is_empty q = True.
In this case, by the definition of pop, pop q should be None. This contradicts our assumption that pop q = Some (v, q1). ...
Step3: Case 2: Assume is_empty q = False.
Here, by the definition of pop, we have pop q = Some (min q, remove_min q). Therefore, from our assumption, it follows that q1 = remove_min q...
Step4: Unfold the values Function for q1: Using the definition of values, we have: values (q1) = values (remove_min q) = map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq...
Step5: By the properties of the Abs_pq constructor and the alist_of function, particularly the inverse relationship between Abs_pq and alist_of, it
follows that: map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq (tl (alist_of q)))) = map fst (tl (alist_of q)) Hence, values(q1) = map fst (tl (alist_of q)).
Step7: Distinctness and the Minimum Element:
Since the alist_of q list is distinct and sorted, the first element hd (alist_of q) is not in the tail of the list tl (alist_of q).Therefore, the first component ..
Step8: Conclusion: Combining the above results, we conclude that v \<notin> set (values q1), which completes the proof.

a2:"v ∈ set |q|"
shows "the (priority q (min q)) ≤ the (priority q v)"

## Informal proof
Proof Step:
To prove this theorem, we proceed step by step:
Step 1: Definitions Recap:
Let′s recap the definitions of the relevant functions and properties:
- min Function: The min function returns the minimum value in the priority queue q. It is defined as `min q = fst (hd (alist_of q))`. Here, `hd` ...
Step 2: Proof Breakdown:
To prove the theorem, we need to show that the priority of the minimum value in q is less than or equal to the priority of v.
Step 3: Case Analysis:
We will perform a case analysis based on the assumption a1: `\<not> is_empty q`. Since q is not empty, there are two possible cases:
Case 1: q is empty: If q is empty according to the assumption, then it contradicts the premise that v is in the set of values of q (a2: `v \<in> set |q|`). ...
Case 2: q is not empty:If q is not empty, then the minimum value in q can be obtained using the min function: `min q`. Let′s denote this minimum ....
Step 4: Priority of m and v:
We want to show that the priority of m is less than or equal to the priority of v. We can express this as: `the (priority q m) \<le> the (priority q v)`.
Step 5: Unfolding priority:
Using the definition of the priority function, we can rewrite the inequality as: `the (map_of (alist_of q) m) \<le> the (map_of (alist_of q) v)`.
Step 6: Set Membership:
Since v is in the set of values of q according to the assumption a2, it implies that v is present in the list representation of q, which is given by ...
Step 7: Map_of Behavior:The map_of function returns None if the key v is not
